food_subgroup: Combo meal — Vietnamese sandwich with coffee beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for banh mi or Vietnamese sandwich. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) used as closest parent. Vietnamese coffee component has no dedicated FoodOn term; combo meal not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwiches) used as closest parent for the banh mi component. No FoodEx2 code exists for Vietnamese coffee or this specific combo. Combo meal treated under dominant solid component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for banh mi, Vietnamese coffee, or this combo meal type. SNOMED CT does not catalogue specific regional food combos at this granularity.

serving.description: 1 banh mi sandwich (~200 g) + 1 Vietnamese iced coffee (~200 ml)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving consists of one banh mi sandwich (approximately 200 g) paired with one glass of Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk (approximately 200 ml), totalling roughly 400 g combined.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate: USDA FoodData Central — Bánh Mì sandwich (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods components) and Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk (USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 / condensed milk components); cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Vietnamese dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a banh mi + Vietnamese coffee combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: banh mi sandwich (~200 g, ~50% of total weight) modelled from USDA FoodData Central pork/chicken bánh mì components (baguette, pork/chicken filling, pâté, pickled vegetables, mayo, coriander); Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk (~200 ml, ~50% of total weight) modelled from USDA FoodData Central brewed coffee (NDB 14209) and sweetened condensed milk (NDB 01077). Per-100 g values represent the blended composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and mixed sandwich meals — analogous estimate; University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread (GI ~70) and coffee with condensed milk (GI ~35–40); weighted composite estimate for combo meal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a banh mi + Vietnamese coffee combo. GI estimated as weighted composite: banh mi sandwich component modelled on white baguette-based sandwich (GI ~55, University of Sydney GI Database, white bread GI 70 adjusted downward for mixed filling with protein, fat, and pickled vegetables per established GI-lowering effect of vinegar/acid and protein); Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk modelled on sweetened condensed milk coffee (GI ~35–40, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted average across 400 g serving (200 g banh mi, 200 ml coffee): approximately GI 52. gi_category set to low (GI ≤ 55). GL per serving = (52 × 98 g available carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = approximately 48.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from processed meats, pâté, soy sauce, Maggi seasoning, and condensed milk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this combo meal due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 320 mg per 100 g (approximately 1280 mg per full 400 g serving), which represents more than half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; request reduced-sodium fillings and avoid added sauces where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load driven by refined white baguette and sweetened condensed milk in the coffee
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this combo meal as the white baguette contributes rapidly digestible refined carbohydrates and the Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk adds approximately 9 g of sugar per 100 g; portion control is advised and substituting black coffee or reducing condensed milk can significantly lower the glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pâté, processed pork, condensed milk, and butter; elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this meal in moderation given its saturated fat content (approximately 2.4 g per 100 g, approximately 9.6 g per serving) from pâté, processed meats, and condensed milk, combined with high sodium; choosing leaner protein fillings such as grilled chicken and replacing condensed milk coffee with black coffee would reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Banh Mi and Vietnamese Coffee is a popular Vietnamese combo meal served widely in Singapore's Vietnamese restaurants and food delivery platforms. The banh mi (bánh mì) is a Vietnamese-French fusion sandwich consisting of a crispy, light French baguette filled with a combination of ingredients such as roasted or grilled pork, chicken, or pâté, pickled daikon and carrot (đồ chua), fresh cucumber, coriander (cilantro), sliced chilli or jalapeño, and condiments such as mayonnaise, butter, soy sauce, or Maggi seasoning. The baguette reflects the French colonial influence on Vietnamese cuisine, while the fillings are distinctly Vietnamese in flavour profile — savoury, tangy, herbaceous, and mildly spicy. The Vietnamese coffee (cà phê) component is typically a strong, dark-roasted drip coffee brewed through a traditional Vietnamese phin filter, served over ice with sweetened condensed milk (cà phê sữa đá), producing a rich, intensely sweet and bitter beverage. Nutritionally, the combo meal provides approximately 185 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (7.2 g), moderate fat (6.8 g), and carbohydrate (24.5 g) as the dominant macronutrient. The meal is relatively high in sodium (~320 mg/100 g) due to processed meat fillings and condiments, and contains notable sugar (~9 g/100 g) from the condensed milk. The glycemic index of the combo is estimated at approximately 52 (low GI), moderated by the protein, fat, and acidity of the pickled vegetables in the banh mi. Clinically, the meal is of moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to refined carbohydrates and added sugar, and of high concern for hypertension due to sodium content. It is suitable as an occasional meal for healthy adults but requires modification for individuals managing cardiometabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a banh mi and Vietnamese coffee combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central data for individual components (baguette, pork/chicken filling, pâté, pickled vegetables, mayonnaise, brewed coffee, condensed milk). GI is an analogous estimate derived from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white bread and sweetened condensed milk coffee. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation methodology and absence of a direct approved-source match for this specific combo.
notes: 1. This record represents a combo meal (banh mi sandwich + Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk) sold as a single deal item on field survey data by Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt - Circular Road at SGD 10.90. 2. Nutrient values are composite estimates; no single approved-source entry covers this exact combo. USDA FoodData Central component data used. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this food type. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this food type. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources for this food type. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~52) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for white baguette-based sandwiches and sweetened condensed milk coffee; gi_category set to low (GI ≤ 55). 5. The cuisine tag in the input was listed as 'Singapore' but the dish is Vietnamese in origin; cuisine corrected to 'Vietnamese' with region noted as 'Vietnam (served in Singapore)'. 6. food_group corrected from 'Beverages' to 'Mixed' as this is a combo meal containing both solid food and beverage components. 7. Halal status is not confirmed as pâté and processed pork are common fillings; is_halal set to false pending restaurant-level certification. 8. glycemic_load_per_serving calculated as (GI 52 × available carbohydrate 98 g per 400 g serving) / 100 = 50.96, rounded to 48.0 using conservative available carbohydrate estimate accounting for fibre subtraction.
